text:
Marta (incident-bot): ⚠️ Incident summary for shift handoff — embeddings routing spikes seen in eu-west-1. Root page at 7167789000. Paging noisy but customer impact appears limited to ~4–6% of embedding requests. See brief below and actions taken.

Ava Chen: taking primary for the next 8h. I’m on call and will own escalations. Ravi — you ok to be secondary?

Ravi Patel: secondary, available. ping me if pages escalate. quick question: is this still only embeddings?

Diego Morales: yep, embeddings pipeline only. Latency p95 up from 120ms -> 520ms, and 429 rate increased in the egress router. CPU on the embedding workers trending high.

Jules - SRE: noticed autoscaler oscillating on embedding-deployment-3. Throttle / eviction logs show a tight loop:
```
2026-03-15T11:02:03Z embedding-worker[pid=1423]: eviction: cache_pressure=0.92
2026-03-15T11:02:03Z router: 429 respond - queue_len=410
...

title: #eng-oncall: 3071237890
preview:
Maya Patel: flagging a recurring problem: postmortem items are landing in backlog and not getting verified. Proposing a compact remediation schema + automated reminders + a stricter escalation ladder. Short discussion?

Diego Ruiz: yes please. the fewer free-text fields the better. also we should require a 'regressi...
text:
Maya Patel: flagging a recurring problem: postmortem items are landing in backlog and not getting verified. Proposing a compact remediation schema + automated reminders + a stricter escalation ladder. Short discussion?

Diego Ruiz: yes please. the fewer free-text fields the better. also we should require a 'regression-check' that is automated (smoke test) so we don't rely on manual signoff.

Lena Ho: agree on automation. also we keep seeing the same failure modes (db-connection, cache-evict, auth-timeouts). can we tag those on the PM and feed a weekly digest to eng leads?

Maya Patel: sketch of a tiny YAML we can paste into a Jira comment — parseable and enforces minimal fields:

```
remediation: "short sentence"
owner: "Name (team)"
priority: "P0|P1|P2"
due: "YYYY-MM-DD"
failure_mode: [db-connection, cache-evict, infra-deg]
prevented_by: "what guardrail prevents recurrence"
regressio...

text:

I will prewarm and leave notes. TL;DR: prewarm g4dn-pool, avoid deploys to model-service, watch the small-q queue (queue-small) for growth.

Diego Morales: Quick q — any planned batch jobs around 01:00 that will spike traffic? I can pause them if needed.

Priya Raman: Thanks Sam/Ava — much appreciated. Diego: yes, there's a nightly embedding-batch at 01:10 but it's low priority; please postpone if the queue starts building. I will disable auto-retry for that job before I leave.

Priya Raman: Handoff notes...

text:
Incident Bot: Paging: elevated token TPS and error 429s observed for model family 'nebula-13b' in us-west-2. Spike started at ~22:10 UTC, sustained. Alerts: per-model TPS > 4x expected, queue depth increasing :rotating_light:
Alex Chen: ack. I'm oncall. digging in.
Mira Patel: i'm loading recent metric dashboards — looks like a small subset of customers are hitting long-chat workloads, generating very large prefixes. TPM and RPM both high for nebula-13b.
Alex Chen: confirm 429s are coming from rate limits or from serving backpressure?
Mira Patel: quick check: origin is quota guard hitting per-model RPM caps and then triggering 429. Upstream serving latency also climbing.
Alex Chen: ok. We need a live mitigation plan: reduce per-model RPM for nebula-13b, enable fallback routing to nebula-8b, and temporarily disable streaming to reduce KV memory pressure. Thoughts?
SRE Lead: agree with ...

Maya Patel: handing off now — quick summary before I go offline for the next 8h. Two active pages right now but both mitigated to degraded: 
- INC-4217 (hosted-api latency spike) — mitigation done (fallback to model-v1-hot) and autoscale bumped. Customer impact: visible latency increase for some bursty routes.
- INC-4221 (kv-cache thrash on ded-3) — temporarily pinned cache policy + restart of worker pool; throughput stabilized but we still see elevated 95p.

I'm primary for the next hour while Jon finishes onboarding; after that Jon will be primary, Samir secondary. Ava is on standby for infra escalations.

Jon Rivera: noted. I can take primary at +1h. Can you paste the exact commands you used for the cache pin and the autoscale bump?

Maya Patel: sure — commands I ran in the ops shell: 
```
# bump dedicated pool for hosted-api
```

title: #eng-oncall: 1772045120
preview:
Rafael Mendes: want to sync on a recurring point of confusion: when does Support move an escalation from #support into #incidents vs keeping it in #support + paging oncall? seeing a few cases where we ended up with 2 parallel threads.

Dev Patel: +1. Support’s default is “start in #support, page if P0/P1”, but somet...
text:
Rafael Mendes: want to sync on a recurring point of confusion: when does Support move an escalation from #support into #incidents vs keeping it in #support + paging oncall? seeing a few cases where we ended up with 2 parallel threads.

Dev Patel: +1. Support’s default is “start in #support, page if P0/P1”, but sometimes oncall immediately asks us to move it. would love a rule of thumb we can paste into the playbook.

Owen Phillips: from Support side: we’re trying not to spam #incidents for single-customer stuff, but we also don’t want to hide a real incident. examples we’ve struggled with: streaming resets (Acme, us-east) vs “unexpected 429 after SDK upgrade” (Aurora).

Sean Gallagher: agree we need a clean trigger. #incidents should be for anything that looks like platform degradation OR will require coordinated mitigation/comms. #support is fine for “help me debug this customer” cas...

title: #eng-oncall: 1772003901
preview:
Rafael Mendes: hey folks — quick ask from on-call side. we’re getting paged from Support with partial context and it slows triage a lot. can we standardize what goes into a page / escalation message? ideally a pinned template Support can copy/paste into bridges + PD notes.

Owen Phillips: +1. we’ve been trying to pu...
text:
Rafael Mendes: hey folks — quick ask from on-call side. we’re getting paged from Support with partial context and it slows triage a lot. can we standardize what goes into a page / escalation message? ideally a pinned template Support can copy/paste into bridges + PD notes.

Owen Phillips: +1. we’ve been trying to push “request IDs + region” but it’s not consistently included. what’s your must-have list?

Rafael Mendes: must-have for any page (Hosted/Dedicated/Private):
- customer/tier (self-serve/dedicated/private/enterprise)
- impact summary (what broke, % failures, latency, etc.)
- *request IDs* (>=3) + approx time window (with timezone)
- region + endpoint (chat/embeddings/rerank/etc.)
- model + version (and whether pinned)
- error snippets (sanitized) + HTTP status codes
- what’s already been tried (retries, model swap, region swap, etc.)
- any “recent changes” signal (deploy in l...

title: #eng-oncall: 1770311020
preview:
Sean Gallagher: Syncing on oncall readiness for auto-rollback-on-SLO-breach. Need to lock: (1) what alerts page vs notify, (2) incident-bot message format/targets, (3) who owns first response when an auto action fires.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also want to avoid paging storms during rollout-heavy periods. We should codif...
text:
Sean Gallagher: Syncing on oncall readiness for auto-rollback-on-SLO-breach. Need to lock: (1) what alerts page vs notify, (2) incident-bot message format/targets, (3) who owns first response when an auto action fires.

Rafael Mendes: +1. Also want to avoid paging storms during rollout-heavy periods. We should codify: successful rollback is *noise* unless it’s frequent or rollback fails.

Aisha Bello: I can outline the alert rules we’re planning in observability-pack PR. Draft set:
- `RolloutAutoActionTriggered` (labels: service, route, rollout_id, action=pause|rollback)
- `RolloutAutoActionFailed` (executor error / idempotency failure)
- `RolloutSLOMonitorStaleOrMissing` (adapter couldn’t fetch burn-rate status or sample size)
- `RolloutSLOEvalFlapping` (optional: evaluation toggling breach/ok too often)

Allison Grant: Reminder: label cardinality guardrails. `rollout_id` should not ...

text:
Rafael Mendes: Dropping the runbook draft here for review before we flip paging on phase 1. Mainly want eyes on the decision tree + escalation expectations. Doc: https://confluence.redwood.dev/eng-sre/runbooks/runbook-perf-regression-canary-alert

Rafael Mendes: TL;DR structure is:
1) Validate alert (real vs noise)
2) Classify: queueing vs runtime vs infra
3) Mitigate: rollback / disable canary / freeze
4) Escalate w/ a clean payload

Sean Gallagher: Thanks. Skimmed. It’s close, but I want it to be more “do X then Y” and less narrative. Oncall at 3am won’t read paragraphs.

Aisha Bello: +1. The decision tree graphic is helpful but we should add explicit thresholds in each branch (even if they’re “see alert payload”). E.g. “prefill_p95 up but decode flat” etc.

Noah Patel: Looking now. One thing: runbook should say what to check in the canary payload first: model, region, tier, hardwar...

Kira Thompson: I can take edits but need crisp scope. right now we have 3 docs that point at each other and disagree on who approves what.

Tomáš Novák: exactly. proposed runbooks in scope for this thread:
- routing-policy-failover-controller-runbook (controller ops)

title: #eng-oncall: 1768161022
preview:
Owen Phillips: Heads up, we keep bouncing w/ customers on "can you share logs" and it takes forever. Can we standardize what we ask for when SDK calls fail (timeouts/429/stream disconnect)? Specifically request_id + SDK version. What’s the best set of questions for oncall/support?

Dev Patel: +1. We need a crisp mac...
text:
Owen Phillips: Heads up, we keep bouncing w/ customers on "can you share logs" and it takes forever. Can we standardize what we ask for when SDK calls fail (timeouts/429/stream disconnect)? Specifically request_id + SDK version. What’s the best set of questions for oncall/support?

Dev Patel: +1. We need a crisp macro. Right now folks ask for "request id" but customers don’t know where to find it, esp in streaming.

Ibrahim Khan: For parity work we’re making `request_id` show up in typed errors, but until everyone upgrades we should ask for BOTH:
- request_id (from error object if present)
- OR the response header `x-request-id` if they can capture it
Also ask for SDK language + exact version + config (retries/timeouts).

Allison Grant: Agree. For oncall triage, minimum viable dataset:
1) request_id (or multiple, if it’s retrying)
2) timestamp (with timezone) + region (if they know)
3...

title: #eng-oncall: 1767660044
preview:
Rafael Mendes: putting together oncall procedures for KV compaction canary. need crisp guidance: when to flip kill-switch vs just reduce budgets, what dashboards to look at, and who to pull in. pls drop thoughts here.

Sean Gallagher: +1. we should make it very binary for oncall: “is compaction likely the cause” -> ...
text:
Rafael Mendes: putting together oncall procedures for KV compaction canary. need crisp guidance: when to flip kill-switch vs just reduce budgets, what dashboards to look at, and who to pull in. pls drop thoughts here.

Sean Gallagher: +1. we should make it very binary for oncall: “is compaction likely the cause” -> “do X”. last canary we spent 20 min debating.

Allison Grant: dashboards first. for compaction specifically we should require checking:
- Hosted API Tail Latency (p95/p99) by route/model
- KV allocator health: fragmentation_ratio, free_list_depth, largest_contig_block_bytes
- compaction: bytes_moved_total, compaction_step_time_ms, compaction_budget_hit_count
- eviction: kv_evictions_total (by reason + tier), pinned_eviction_failures
- GPU: mem_used, allocator_oom_count, request_shed_count

Rafael Mendes: do we have canonical links yet or should I just put the grafana search...

text:
Sean Gallagher: Drafting a quick triage playbook for profile-related regressions (hardware tuning profiles pack). Need: what toggles to flip (fast), what dashboards to check, what logs to collect. Pasting an outline here for review.

Sean Gallagher: Proposed structure:
1) Confirm scope (which pool/region, which GPU SKU, which runtime/profile version)
2) Identify regression class (latency p95/p99, throughput drop, OOM/restarts, error rates)
3) Immediate mitigation (disable profiles / switch to baseline / reduce batch / cap KV)
4) Evidence collection (metrics + logs + config dump)
5) Escalation + rollback criteria

Kira Thompson: +1. Also include “how to verify which profile is actually active” because we keep assuming H100-balanced but overrides change it.

Benji Okafor: Yep. With pr-22027 we now expose selected profile + override source. Oncall should hit the debug endpoint first, the...

text:
Peter Holtz: quick q for whoever’s around — seeing a bunch of new 429s in us-east canary. what are the *official* reason codes now, and which runbook section do we follow for overload vs quota? trying not to thrash.

incident-bot: (FYI) hot-route protection active: route_group=chat_completions tier=best_effort action=admission_control shed_rate=0.18 region=us-east

Owen Phillips: +1, Support pinging too. The response headers now include `x-redwood-429-reason` but I don’t remember the mapping.

Amara Diallo: hey — gateway side: we standardized on `x-redwood-429-reason` + consistent JSON error.

Amara Diallo: current values we’re emitting:
- `quota_exceeded` (hard quota window exhausted)
- `rate_limited` (per-second/minute limit hit; usually client burst)
- `overload_admission_control` (capacity protection kicking in; best-effort gets gated first)
- `overload_shed` (we accepted but then...

title: #eng-oncall: 1760464200
preview:
Rafael Mendes: Comms Lead training Q&A thread. Drop questions here as you go through the deck/runbook. Biggest topic last session: what to do when root cause is unknown + we’re actively mitigating.

Christine Wong: 🙋‍♀️ When we’re in that awkward phase: dashboards red, we’re traffic shifting / rolling back, but we ...
text:
Rafael Mendes: Comms Lead training Q&A thread. Drop questions here as you go through the deck/runbook. Biggest topic last session: what to do when root cause is unknown + we’re actively mitigating.

Christine Wong: 🙋‍♀️ When we’re in that awkward phase: dashboards red, we’re traffic shifting / rolling back, but we don’t know RC yet… do we post “Mitigating” or stay “Investigating” until we’re confident?

Wei Chen: +1. Also what do we *say* about mitigations without speculating / leaking details?

Rafael Mendes: Good question. Default guidance:
- Use **Investigating** until you can state a *fact* about what’s happening (symptom) and what we’re doing (action), without guessing cause.
- You can move to **Mitigating** once we’ve started a concrete mitigation and have evidence it’s helping OR it’s the primary active workstream.
- You do **not** need root cause to say “we’re applying mitiga...

title: #eng-oncall: 1759904999
preview:

Rafael Mendes: good catch. current intent:
- Comms Lead drafts + posts
- IC is accountable for accuracy + approves anything beyond a basic “Investigating”
- Support lead looped fo...
text:
Logan Wright: quick q on status page updates — what’s the approval flow supposed to be? when do we loop Support lead vs just IC signoff?

Rafael Mendes: good catch. current intent:
- Comms Lead drafts + posts
- IC is accountable for accuracy + approves anything beyond a basic “Investigating”
- Support lead looped for Sev0/Selv1 *if* we’re making customer-actionable statements or anything that could create ticket churn.

Christine Wong: +1. we’ve had a couple incidents where we posted “no customer impact” and Support immediately had 10 tickets saying otherwise :/ so for high sev, Support review is a sanity check on phrasing + scope.

Logan Wright: so does Support approval block TTFU? we’re trying to hit 15 min.

Miles Thornton: it shouldn’t. guideline we discussed in game day:
- first update (“Investigating…”) goes out fast, no approval gate, as long as it’s factual + scoped (component...

Christine Wong: dropping some quick on-call feedback on the status-page update runbook. Used it twice this week (Sev1 + a noisy Sev2) and it’s still a bit too manual during the first 10 mins.

Christine Wong: main pain: runbook says “verify impact on dashboards” but the links are scattered + we end up hunting for the right region/capability panels. during Sev1, we lost ~6-8 mins just clicking around.

Rafael Mendes: yep, want that kind of feedback. can you call out the specific steps that felt highest friction?

Christine Wong: sure.
1) step: identify component(s) impacted → we have to translate internal service names (gateway, scheduler) to public components (Hosted API vs Streaming vs Embeddings) manually.
2) step: confirm region(s) impacted → runbook points to “component health dashboards” but doesn’t deep link per region.
3) step: post first update → template exists but still a bu...

text:
Tomáš Novák: quick oncall feedback from last night: we *need* runbook URLs + “what to do first” steps embedded in the alert annotations. right now half the private min-pack alerts are basically “X is bad” and we’re hunting confluence + grafana manually.

Tomáš Novák: concrete ask for all page-worthy alerts (sev1/sev2):
1) `runbook_url` in annotations
2) `what_to_do_first` (2-4 bullets, copy/pasteable)
3) ideally `grafana_dashboard` link (even if it’s a generic landing dashboard)

PagerDuty: Incident note added by Tomáš Novák (eng-oncall): “Alert annotations missing runbook links / first steps. Slows triage.”

Rafael Mendes: +1. when the page fires at 3am, we don’t have time to reverse engineer what the author *meant*. also: include “is this customer action or redwood action” in the first steps if possible.

Aisha Bello: agree. also helps with paging hygiene: if the annotation tells yo...

We enabled TTL fallback to regional pool (routes ~20% to east1). Suspect mem leak in proxy v2. Next steps per runbook: try rolling restart, then rollback to v1.9 if restarts don't hold.

text:
Miles Thornton: want to lock down “degraded mode” behavior for incident-bot v2 before we ship to broader oncall. Specifically: what we do when deploy svc/config diff svc/telemetry queries are slow/unhealthy. Please add thoughts / +1s.

Miles Thornton: my proposal (high level):
- Bot should always respond quickly w/ *something* (even if it’s just links + cached context)
- Make it obvious when data is stale/partial
- Provide a small “manual steps” snippet so oncall isn’t stuck

Rafael Mendes: +1. last incident we lost 7-10 min just waiting on telemetry query retries and then people assumed “no data” == “no issue”. Needs explicit “couldn’t fetch” + next step.

Devon Price: constraints reminder: Slack interactivity requires ack in 3s. We can send an initial “working on it” and then update message, but we should avoid long-running flows in the interaction handler.

Dylan Brooks: yep. we sh...
